name: Umberhold
overview: A muddy, decaying village that serves as the opening hub. Gravenhold Manor looms to the northwest, the graveyard stirs to the east, and the village well has become a deadly immediate threat.

difficulty_signposting.message: Dangerous, but the clearest first move.
Available early, but deadlier than it first appears.
Immediate combat set piece; very dangerous if attempted too early.

locations.name: Umberhold Inn
locations.summary: The party's safest base in the village and the only reliable save point.

services.label: Rest
services.notes: Free if a Troubadour is in the party.

locations.name: Iron Shovel & Scutage
locations.summary: General gear shop, item identification service, and weapon silvering service.

services.label: Silver One-Handed Melee Weapon
services.notes: Oswaldine does not sell pre-silvered weapons. She silvers a one-handed melee weapon the party already owns.

services.label: Silver Two-Handed Melee Weapon
services.notes: Oswaldine does not sell pre-silvered weapons. She silvers a two-handed melee weapon the party already owns.

locations.name: Solcine Chantry
locations.summary: Healing, holy goods, and resurrection services.

locations.name: Alchemist's Mortar
locations.summary: Locked at game start. Inspecting it begins the missing alchemist thread.
interaction_rules: Inspecting the note starts The Missing Alchemist.
The shop remains closed until Etheldreda is rescued.

locations.name: The Bailiff's Desk
locations.summary: Primary quest dispatch point for village troubles.

locations.name: Village Well
locations.summary: A rope-and-bucket descent directly into a troll encounter below the well.
interaction_rules: The party may descend using the rope and bucket rig.
Descending hands off directly to combat.
There is no well dungeon to explore.
After victory, the party climbs back up.

locations.name: East Graveyard
locations.summary: A disturbed graveyard whose rear mausoleum stair leads to a single-level 26x26 dungeon below.
interaction_rules: The rear mausoleum contains the descent stair.
The dungeon below is one level only.
The graveyard content is intended to be deadlier than the manor route.

locations.name: South Gates
locations.summary: The village exit remains shut until the manor threat is broken below ground.

npcs.name: Bartholomew
npcs.summary: A weary innkeeper with kind eyes and a cautious voice.

default: Keep your voice low… walls carry sound these days.
Used to be folk laughed here. Now they listen. Always listening.
after_missing_alchemist: Word travels fast. Etheldreda’s back, and that’s more good news than we’ve had in months.
after_restless_dead: Heard bells at the graveyard this morning. Proper mourning bells.
after_troll_trouble: Water’s flowing and the buckets are filling. Strange how hopeful that sounds.
after_all_three: Still wouldn’t call this place safe. But I’d call it living.

npcs.name: Oswaldine
npcs.summary: A hard-faced trader with a sharp tongue, sharper instincts, and the skill to silver a weapon you already own.

default: Gold talks. If you’ve got it, I’ve got something worth your trouble.
I don’t sell silvered blades off the rack. Bring me the weapon and the coin, and I’ll do it proper.
If you’re going down that shaft, buy oil. And if you’re clever, fire.
after_missing_alchemist: Good. The alchemist’s alive. Means I can stop getting asked for brews I don’t stock.
after_restless_dead: East side’s calmed down some. Grim trade is still trade, but panic’s worse.
after_troll_trouble: Water trade’s back. Turns out civilization matters.
after_all_three: Village still limps. But it’s limping forward now.

npcs.name: Acolyte Gidney
npcs.summary: Soft-spoken but resolute. One of the few calm presences in Umberhold.

default: Solace watches, even here… though her light feels distant.
The earth there is troubled. The dead are not at rest.
after_missing_alchemist: One life returned is no small mercy.
after_restless_dead: The graveyard is quieter now. Not silent—but no longer pleading.
after_troll_trouble: Clean water is a blessing most only notice when it is threatened.
after_all_three: The darkness has thinned, but it has not withdrawn.

npcs.name: Wymarc
npcs.summary: A stout, restless man buried in parchment and worry.
npcs.suspicious_detail: He becomes tense and avoids eye contact when Etheldreda is mentioned.

default: Ah—new faces. Good. Means you’ve not been scared off yet… or eaten.
Manor’s the heart of it, I think. Graveyard’s uglier. Well’s deadlier.
after_missing_alchemist: You brought her back. Folk needed this win.
after_restless_dead: East side can bury their dead again. That matters.
after_troll_trouble: Water’s ours again. Means no more pigs down a hole, and no more pretending tribute is strategy.
after_all_three: You’ve done more for Umberhold than any order I’ve given in months.

npcs.name: Warden Halvric
npcs.summary: An aging soldier with a rigid sense of duty.

default: Not safe beyond the walls. Not anymore.
Road south looks open, if you don’t know better.

npcs.name: Etheldreda
npcs.summary: Missing at game start; unlocked as a potion vendor after rescue.

default: You have my thanks… though I’d have preferred to meet you under less infernal circumstances.
after_restless_dead: I can smell grave-soil less strongly on the wind now.
after_troll_trouble: Clean water improves every brew I make.
after_all_three: Perhaps I shall hang a proper sign again. Open, maybe.

quests.title: The Missing Alchemist
quests.source_summary: Wymarc suspects Etheldreda was taken near Gravenhold Manor and did not leave willingly.
quests.journal_summary_incomplete: Etheldreda, Umberhold’s alchemist, has vanished. The locked shop and Wymarc’s unease point toward Gravenhold Manor.
quests.journal_summary_complete: Etheldreda was rescued from beneath Gravenhold Manor after being forced to brew for the Boggart Boss.

objectives.text: Defeat the Boggart Boss beneath the manor.

quests.hidden_truth: Etheldreda is held beneath the manor and forced to brew strengthening potions.

quests.title: The Restless Dead
quests.source_summary: The graveyard east of the village no longer lets the dead rest.
quests.journal_summary_incomplete: Something has disturbed the graveyard east of Umberhold. A dark stair in the rear mausoleum seems to lead below to the source.
quests.journal_summary_complete: The corruption beneath Umberhold’s graveyard has been broken and the site can be mourned in peace again.

objectives.text: Descend the dark stair in the rear mausoleum.

quests.title: Troll Trouble
quests.source_summary: Trolls below the village well have seized the water source and forced tribute from the villagers.
quests.journal_summary_incomplete: Trolls have taken the village well and the water below it. If the party descends with the rope and bucket rig, they will enter immediate combat.
quests.journal_summary_complete: The trolls below the well are dead and the village water is safe again.

objectives.text: Descend into the well using the rope and bucket.
Kill the trolls below and climb back out.
